subject: Computer Networking
title: WebSockets for Real-Time
description: A fast breakdown of WebSockets for Real-Time for builders.

hook.text: HTTP is request-response. But chat apps need the server to push messages instantly. WebSockets do that.

buildup.text: A WebSocket starts as an HTTP request, then upgrades to a persistent two-way connection.

discovery.text: Once open, both sides send messages freely. No request needed. Data flows in real time, low latency.

twist.text: WebSockets stay open for hours. HTTP connections close after each response. Different lifetime, different use.

climax.text: Stock tickers, multiplayer games, and collaborative editors all rely on WebSocket connections.

punchline.text: WebSockets keep the line open. Real-time web, no polling.

quiz.question: How does a WebSocket connection begin?
options: As an HTTP request that upgrades to a persistent connection
As a direct TCP socket bypassing HTTP entirely
As a UDP packet sent to the server
